companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: NVDA trading at approximately $197.58 on July 2 with no major NVIDIA-specific announcements today. The Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System (announced June 29) continues to drive government AI momentum. CoreWeave's first Vera Rubin NVL72 production validation (June 1) established the benchmark for next-generation AI cloud infrastructure. Anthropic's Fable 5 restoration on July 1 accelerates full Claude traffic to Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters.

items.headline: NVDA at ~$197.58 — Palantir Sovereign AI OS momentum carries into July 2
items.detail: NVIDIA stock held approximately $197.58 on July 2 as markets process the Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System announced June 29. The system runs NVIDIA Nemotron open models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra infrastructure for U.S. government agencies, giving customers ownership of model weights and training data. PLTR surged 9% on July 1 on the news while NVDA was roughly flat, reflecting investors' view that Palantir captures the software premium while NVIDIA's hardware value is already priced in at current levels.

items.headline: BioNeMo Agent Toolkit live; record 35 European Vera Rubin HPC systems in development
items.detail: At ISC High Performance 2026 in Hamburg, NVIDIA launched the BioNeMo Agent Toolkit enabling AI agents to gather evidence, reason across scientific findings, run computational experiments, and accelerate drug discovery. A record 35 AI HPC systems using Vera Rubin are in development across European research institutions. NVIDIA technology now runs 81% of TOP500 supercomputers with 90% of new entries built on NVIDIA — reinforcing supply dominance as Vera Rubin ramps.

items.headline: CoreWeave completes first Vera Rubin NVL72 validation — production benchmark established
items.detail: CoreWeave completed a 147-hour validation suite and became the first AI cloud to bring NVIDIA's Vera Rubin NVL72 systems into production on June 1, delivering 4x better training and 10x improved inference per watt compared to prior-generation systems. This validation serves as the production baseline for all Vera Rubin deployments. Fable 5's restoration on July 1 accelerates full Claude workload utilization on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters after a 19-day suspension.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 2 with no confirmed date — it was targeted for June, then 'July,' with no specific day announced. GOOGL trades around $357, down approximately 12% from its May 18 all-time high of $408.61. Alphabet joined the Dow Jones Industrial Average on June 29. DeepMind's talent exodus — Noam Shazeer to OpenAI, John Jumper to Anthropic, and three other senior researchers — continues to weigh on sentiment and Apple's September Siri AI launch timeline.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro still unreleased as of July 2 — no confirmed release date
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro, announced at Google I/O and originally targeted for June GA, remains unreleased as of July 2. Google is refining coding performance, multi-step reasoning, and token efficiency before general release. Pricing is expected around $2 per million input tokens and $12 per million output tokens. The delay maintains delivery risk for Apple's Siri AI September 2026 launch, which depends on a refined Gemini model, and compounds investor concerns following DeepMind's talent exodus in June.

items.headline: Alphabet added to Dow Jones Industrial Average — stock +4% on June 29
items.detail: Alphabet officially entered the DJIA on June 29, replacing Verizon, and rose approximately 4% on the news. Q1 2026 Google Cloud revenue was up 63% year over year with a contracted backlog of $460 billion. The Dow inclusion does not change AI product fundamentals but signals institutional commitment to Alphabet as a benchmark holding. GOOGL trades around $357 on July 2, recovering from the $408 all-time high set in May.

items.headline: DeepMind talent exodus: Noam Shazeer to OpenAI; John Jumper active at Anthropic
items.detail: Noam Shazeer — Gemini co-lead and Transformer paper co-author — departed DeepMind for OpenAI in June 2026 in what is shaping up as the most significant AI talent reallocation of the year. John Jumper (Nobel laureate, AlphaFold2 co-creator) is now active at Anthropic, making his first public appearance at Anthropic's AI for Science virtual event on June 30. Three other senior researchers also departed (Jonas Adler, Alexander Pritzel, Arthur Conmy). The exodus cost Alphabet approximately $225 billion in market cap during a single-day 5% drop in June.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft 365 commercial pricing increases are now in full effect following the July 1 rollout. Copilot Cowork usage-based billing at $0.01 per Copilot Credit is active and in its first full commercial billing cycle. Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra are generally available. Reports indicate Microsoft is evaluating DeepSeek V4 as a cost-efficient option within Copilot Cowork alongside OpenAI and Anthropic models.

items.headline: Microsoft 365 with Copilot is now a permanent standard commercial SKU
items.detail: Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot ($23.50/user/month) and Business Premium with Copilot ($32/user/month) became permanent standard SKUs on July 1, converting Copilot from an optional add-on to a baseline commercial tier across Enterprise, Business, and Frontline segments. This is Microsoft's largest monetization step for its AI investment, pushing Copilot revenue to baseline across millions of commercial subscribers. Microsoft plans a 40% GB300 capacity expansion on Azure by September 2026.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork usage billing active — multi-model architecture confirmed as long-term direction
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork — generally available since June 16 — is in its first full commercial billing cycle at $0.01 per Copilot Credit. The Frontier tier supports Anthropic Opus 4.8, Sonnet 4.6, and OpenAI GPT-5.5, cementing multi-model architecture as Microsoft's long-term direction. Work IQ APIs and Microsoft IQ are generally available across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Tenants without configured usage-based billing lost Cowork access on July 1.

items.headline: Microsoft reportedly evaluating DeepSeek V4 for Copilot Cowork cost efficiency
items.detail: Reports indicate Microsoft is weighing the addition of DeepSeek V4 as a cost-saving model option within Copilot Cowork alongside OpenAI and Anthropic offerings. The evaluation reflects Microsoft's stated multi-model strategy, selecting the best model for each task rather than defaulting to a single provider. No official Microsoft announcement has been made as of July 2. Claude models in Microsoft Foundry on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs became generally available June 29.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of July 2. Broader access is expected in the July 10–17 window per Sam Altman's stated intent. OpenAI confirmed GPT-5.6 Sol will deploy on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month at up to 750 tokens per second. The Apple breach-of-contract dispute remains unresolved with outside counsel retained. ChatGPT's global market share fell to 46.4% — the first time below 50%.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol broader access window July 10–17 — government access restrictions continue
items.detail: GPT-5.6 Sol ($5/$30 per million input/output tokens), Terra ($2.50/$15), and Luna ($1/$6) remain in limited government-staged preview as of July 2, restricted to approximately 20 organizations under a White House executive order requiring staged deployment benchmarking. Sam Altman told staff he hopes to release GPT-5.6 broadly around July 10–17. GPT-5.5 remains fully available across all channels. GPT-4.5 was retired June 26 and replaced by GPT-5.5 in existing conversations.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol deploying on Cerebras this month at up to 750 tokens per second
items.detail: OpenAI confirmed plans to deploy GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras wafer-scale hardware this month, targeting up to 750 tokens per second — dramatically faster than GPU-based inference. The OpenAI-Cerebras multi-year compute deal covers 750 megawatts of inference capacity valued at more than $20 billion, with an option to scale to $30 billion. Initial Cerebras Sol access will be limited as capacity expands alongside the broader GPT-5.6 rollout. OpenAI already confirmed Codex-Spark on Cerebras achieves more than 1,000 tokens per second for coding tasks.

items.headline: Apple-OpenAI breach-of-contract dispute — formal notice still pending as of July 2
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and continues to weigh a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over ChatGPT's perceived lack of prime placement in the rebuilt Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed. The dispute is a secondary consequence of Apple's June 8 announcement that Google Gemini — not ChatGPT — powers the rebuilt Siri AI in iOS 27. Both sides are reported to prefer an out-of-court resolution. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% — first time below 50%, with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: July 2 is the first full day Anthropic operates with Fable 5 and Mythos 5 fully restored globally, Claude Sonnet 5 as the new default for Free and Pro, and Claude Science in beta. Claude Science grant applications are open through July 15 with up to $30,000 in compute credits for 50 selected projects. Anthropic's October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target remains intact following the $65B Series H at $965B post-money valuation and $47B ARR.

items.headline: Claude Sonnet 5 is the new default for Free and Pro — first full day in production
items.detail: July 2 is the first full day Claude Sonnet 5 serves as the default model for Anthropic's Free and Pro plans following its July 1 launch. Sonnet 5 offers performance approaching Opus 4.8 at roughly 60% lower cost, at introductory pricing of $2 per million input tokens and $10 per million output tokens through August 31, 2026, then $3/$15 at standard rates. Key agentic capabilities include multi-step planning, browser and terminal tool use, self-verification, and improved large-codebase search. Prompt caching provides up to 90% cost savings; batch processing up to 50%.

items.headline: Claude Science grants open through July 15 — up to $30K for 50 research projects
items.detail: Anthropic's Claude Science — a research AI workbench in beta for Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users on macOS and Linux — opened grant applications for up to $30,000 in Claude credits for 50 selected AI for Science projects. Applications close July 15, 2026; awards announced July 31. Claude Science connects to more than 60 scientific databases and includes prebuilt toolkits for genomics, proteomics, structural biology, and cheminformatics. Nobel laureate John Jumper (AlphaFold2 co-creator) joined Anthropic in June and appeared at the Claude Science launch event.

items.headline: Fable 5 globally restored; Mythos 5 limited to U.S. government clearance — IPO track intact
items.detail: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted export controls on July 1, restoring Fable 5 globally and Mythos 5 to a limited set of U.S. organizations with government clearance. The 19-day suspension (June 12–July 1) was resolved after Anthropic patched an Amazon-discovered jailbreak with a new cybersecurity classifier. For Pro, Max, Team, and select Enterprise plans, Fable 5 is included for up to 50% of weekly usage through July 7, then moves to usage credits. Series H: $65B at $965B post-money; ARR: $47B; October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target with Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta stock closed approximately 9–10% higher on July 1, near $612, following Bloomberg's report that Meta is building 'Meta Compute' to sell excess AI capacity. The Meta Compute cloud business caused CoreWeave (CRWV) to fall 14% to $85.68 on competition fears. Meta One paid subscription ($7.99 Plus / $19.99 Premium) is live across the Family of Apps. Meta's $115–135B capex guidance for 2026 remains unchanged.

items.headline: Meta Compute cloud business — Bloomberg details two-track offering; META +9–10%
items.detail: Bloomberg's July 1 report described Meta Compute as a two-pronged cloud business: selling hosted AI model API access (similar to AWS Bedrock) and selling raw GPU compute capacity (similar to CoreWeave). The division is led by Santosh Janardhan (head of infrastructure), Daniel Gross (Meta Superintelligence Labs), and Dina Powell McCormick (Meta President). Meta stock surged 9–10% to approximately $612. The plan follows SpaceX's similar move to monetize surplus Colossus compute. A Meta spokesperson declined to comment on the Bloomberg report.

items.headline: CoreWeave fell 14% to $85.68 on Meta cloud competition fears; Nebius fell 12%+
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) fell 14% to $85.68 on July 1, its worst day since IPO, on fears Meta could become a powerful competitor in AI cloud. Nebius also fell 12%+ on the same news. Evercore ISI reiterated an Outperform rating and $150 price target on CoreWeave, noting the $21B Meta contract through December 2032 has take-or-pay protections and Meta cannot simply redirect spend. The 37-analyst average Buy rating with a $143.41 average price target remains intact. CRWV is recovering on July 2.

items.headline: Meta One paid subscription live; 2026 capex $115–135B unchanged; Meta Glasses strong demand
items.detail: Meta One paid AI subscription is live across the Family of Apps: Plus at $7.99/month and Premium at $19.99/month, directly competing with ChatGPT Plus and Claude Pro. Initial testing markets included Singapore, Guatemala, and Bolivia before broader rollout. Meta Glasses (launched June 23 at $299–$399) show strong early demand with 69.2% smart glasses market share. Meta's 2026 capex guidance of $115–135 billion remains unchanged.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit Washington DC wrapped July 1 having set the largest single-day federal AI infrastructure commitment in AWS history: a $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework, $1B Forward Deployed Engineering program, and AWS Secret Cloud for Industry. GPT-5.6 Sol on Amazon Bedrock is expected mid-July consistent with the broader staged rollout. xAI's Grok 4.3 is now available on Bedrock with tool calling and streaming support, expanding Bedrock's multi-provider catalog.

items.headline: AWS Summit DC federal AI baseline: ICAMF $1B, ASCI, $1B FDE program
items.detail: AWS Summit Washington DC (June 30–July 1) closed with three major federal commitments: the $1B IC Accelerated Modernization Framework eliminating migration costs for Intelligence Community agencies through October 2030; AWS Secret Cloud for Industry (ASCI) giving defense contractors classified cloud access for the first time; and a $1B Forward Deployed Engineering organization placing thousands of AWS engineers on-site with federal customers. CIA Director John Ratcliffe confirmed CIA will use ICAMF, stating LLMs are already helping analysts surface hidden intelligence.

items.headline: Grok 4.3 now on Amazon Bedrock; GPT-5.6 Sol expected on Bedrock mid-July
items.detail: xAI's Grok 4.3 model is now available on Amazon Bedrock with full tool calling, structured output, and streaming support, expanding Bedrock's third-party model catalog to include Claude (Anthropic), GPT-5.5 (OpenAI), and Grok (xAI). GPT-5.6 Sol on Bedrock is expected in the July 10–17 window consistent with the broader staged rollout. This positions Bedrock as a leading multi-provider inference hub for enterprise customers.

items.headline: Bedrock AgentCore Web Search GA; Project Rainier at ~500K Trainium2 chips resuming full Anthropic load
items.detail: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Web Search — fully managed real-time web grounding for AI agents with cited knowledge and zero data egress — is generally available in US East (N. Virginia). Project Rainier remains live with nearly half a million Trainium2 chips; Fable 5's restoration on July 1 accelerates full Claude workload utilization after the 19-day suspension. The expanded Anthropic-AWS agreement commits more than $100 billion over 10 years for up to 5GW of Trainium capacity.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) closed at $170.86 on July 1, up 4.06%. Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 payments started July 1, with both Colossus 1 (Anthropic, ~$1.25B/month) and Colossus 2 (Reflection AI, $150M/month) now fully committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameters, V9 model) remains in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T parameter model is in final training stages targeting late July. The $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 payments begin — both Colossus facilities fully committed
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) became active July 1 at $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first three months. Combined with Colossus 1 serving Anthropic at approximately $1.25 billion per month, both xAI Colossus facilities are now committed to third-party tenants — raising questions about xAI's own future dedicated compute access for Grok model training.

items.headline: Grok 4.5 (1.5T params, V9) in private beta; 2T model expected late July; monthly cadence planned
items.detail: Grok 4.5 — a 1.5 trillion parameter V9 foundation model trained in part on Cursor AI coding data — is running in private beta inside Tesla and SpaceX. xAI claims near-Opus 4.8 performance in internal evaluations; no independent benchmark has been published. A 2 trillion parameter successor model is in the final stages of training with expected completion in late July 2026. xAI plans to release a new model trained from scratch every month for the remainder of 2026.

items.headline: SPCX at $170.86 (+4.06% July 1); Cursor $60B acquisition Q3 close on track; CSAM unresolved
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) closed at $170.86 on July 1, up 4.06%, but still well below its all-time high of $225.64 on June 16. The $60 billion Cursor acquisition (Anysphere) is on track for Q3 2026 close pending regulatory approvals — Cursor has 2M+ developers and approximately $2.6 billion in annual B2B revenue. Grok adult content generation exceeds 10 billion images per month; xAI engineers acknowledged CSAM generation has no current technical fix, drawing regulatory scrutiny and a $530M litigation reserve in SpaceX's IPO prospectus.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: AVGO shares remain down approximately 16.5% over the past month from post-Q2 earnings disappointment, as investors wanted raised full-year AI guidance rather than maintained targets. Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance stands at approximately $16B; the FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target is unchanged. Jalapeño (OpenAI's first custom ASIC, co-developed with Broadcom in nine months) targets H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Six confirmed custom-silicon customers: Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, OpenAI, and one undisclosed.

items.headline: AVGO down ~16.5% past month — post-Q2 earnings pressure despite strong AI fundamentals
items.detail: Broadcom shares are down approximately 16.5% over the past month following Q2 FY2026 earnings (June 3) that disappointed investors expecting raised full-year guidance. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue reached $10.8 billion (+143% YoY) and total Q2 revenue hit a record $22.2 billion (+48% YoY), but Broadcom maintained rather than raised its FY2027 $100B+ AI target. Stock pressure is market-driven and does not affect the six-customer custom-silicon pipeline or the Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform.

items.headline: Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance ~$16B; FY2026 $56B; FY2027 target $100B+
items.detail: Broadcom guided for approximately $16 billion in AI semiconductor revenue in Q3 FY2026 — a 48% sequential increase from Q2's $10.8 billion. Full-year FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue guidance: $56 billion. FY2027 AI semiconductor revenue target: more than $100 billion. The Apollo/Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) enables more than 20GW of Broadcom XPU compute for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028.

items.headline: Jalapeño custom ASIC co-developed in 9 months; six custom silicon customers confirmed; H2 2026 Stargate deployment
items.detail: Jalapeño — OpenAI's first custom AI accelerator chip, co-developed with Broadcom from design to tape-out in nine months on TSMC advanced process — targets H2 2026 deployment in Stargate data centers. Broadcom CEO Hock Tan confirmed OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer alongside Google, Meta, Microsoft, Anthropic, and one undisclosed customer. Jalapeño is a reticle-sized ASIC designed from the ground up for LLM inference with substantially better performance per watt than current GPU alternatives.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Palantir closed at $125.73 on July 1, up 9%, after the NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System announcement. July 2 sees Palantir consolidating after the rally. The Army NGC2 Foundry deployment is advancing as the company's largest federal program. CEO Alex Karp's CNBC interview calling the AI industry 'effing insane' became widely shared. Q1 2026 revenue: $1.632B (+84.7% YoY) — the highest growth rate in company history.

items.headline: PLTR closed $125.73 (+9%) on July 1 on NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS — consolidating July 2
items.detail: Palantir Technologies closed at $125.73 on July 1 — a new all-time valuation high — after the NVIDIA Sovereign AI Operating System announcement. The system runs NVIDIA Nemotron open models on air-gapped Blackwell Ultra for U.S. government agencies, giving customers ownership of model weights and training data on their own hardware. PLTR had hit a 52-week low of $107.27 on June 25 before the recovery. The stock is consolidating on July 2 as markets digest the partnership's operational implications.

items.headline: Army NGC2 advancing to broad deployment; major 2026 federal contract wins totaling $11.3B+
items.detail: The U.S. Army picked Palantir Foundry as the cloud data layer for NGC2 (Next Generation Command and Control), moving from prototype to broad deployment. Major 2026 federal contract wins: $10B 10-year Army enterprise AI services; $1B 5-year DHS AI platform; $300M USDA deal (April); Treasury Department contract (June 23). Q1 2026 U.S. commercial revenue reached $595M (+133% YoY). Full-year guidance raised to approximately 71% growth.

items.headline: CEO Karp: AI industry is 'effing insane' — positions Palantir-NVIDIA Sovereign OS as alternative
items.detail: Palantir CEO Alex Karp called the AI industry 'effing insane' in a heated CNBC interview on July 1, accusing frontier AI firms of overcharging, exploiting customer data, and jeopardizing U.S. national security. Karp argued enterprises fear the token model: 'they're going to chillax and waste my time with tokens, I'm going to get no value and they're going to get my IP.' The interview coincided with the NVIDIA Sovereign AI OS launch, positioning air-gapped Nemotron deployment as the secure enterprise alternative to frontier API consumption.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 developer beta continues with Gemini-powered Siri AI targeting September 2026 GA. Public beta is expected mid-July 2026. Gemini 3.5 Pro's continued delay as of July 2 maintains delivery risk for Apple's September iPhone launch window. OpenAI has retained outside counsel and is weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice over Siri placement. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option to hedge provider risk.

items.headline: iOS 27 developer beta active; public beta mid-July; Gemini Siri AI GA September 2026
items.detail: iOS 27 developer beta — featuring a fully rebuilt Siri AI powered by Google's Gemini model — has been running since WWDC June 8. Public beta is expected mid-July 2026. The rebuilt Siri includes a dedicated standalone app, Dynamic Island integration, full on-screen awareness, and multi-step task execution through a three-tier system: on-device, Private Cloud Compute, and Gemini for complex tasks. The iOS 27 Extensions framework (user choice of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remains disabled pending EU DMA regulatory clarity. GA: September 2026.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro delay persists as of July 2 — ongoing risk to September Siri AI launch
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 2, maintaining delivery risk for Apple's September 2026 iPhone launch window that depends on a refined Gemini model. Apple's reported $1 billion per year Gemini deal makes the September timeline vulnerable to Google's internal quality delays. DeepMind's talent exodus — Noam Shazeer to OpenAI, John Jumper to Anthropic, plus three others — compounds the delivery risk. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini.

items.headline: OpenAI breach-of-contract notice still pending; Apple evaluating Mistral as additional model
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and continues weighing a formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over the lack of prime ChatGPT placement in Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of July 2. Reports indicate Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri and iOS integration — a multi-provider hedging strategy to avoid reliance on any single AI partner.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD Advancing AI 2026 is 20 days away (July 22–23 at Moscone Center, San Francisco). MI450 samples are confirmed shipping to lead customers OpenAI and Meta. Oracle has announced a 50,000-unit MI450 deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster in Q3 2026. AMD's MI500 series roadmap (CDNA 6, 2nm, HBM4E) targets 1,000x performance vs. MI300X for 2027 production. Q2 2026 financial results are due August 4.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 — July 22–23 at Moscone Center; formal MI450 launch expected
items.detail: AMD Advancing AI 2026 at Moscone Center in San Francisco on July 22–23 is the next major AMD milestone. CEO Lisa Su is expected to formally launch MI450, preview EPYC Venice (Zen 6) CPUs, confirm Helios rack-scale systems, and provide a first look at the MI500 series targeting 2027. Analysts project the MI400 series will generate approximately $7.2 billion in revenue in its first year. Q2 2026 financial results are due August 4.

items.headline: MI450 samples shipping to OpenAI and Meta; Oracle 50K unit deployment Q3 public supercluster
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to lead customers OpenAI (6GW partnership, starting with 1GW MI450 in H2 2026) and Meta ($60B/6GW multi-generation agreement). Oracle announced a 50,000-unit AMD Instinct MI450 GPU deployment for the first publicly available MI450-based AI supercluster, with initial deployment in Q3 2026. MI450 features 432GB HBM4 memory, 20TB/s bandwidth, and 40 PFLOPs FP4 per GPU. Demand has already exceeded AMD's own internal 2027 projections.

items.headline: MI500 series revealed: CDNA 6, 2nm, HBM4E — targeting 1,000x performance vs MI300X for 2027
items.detail: AMD revealed the MI500 series roadmap targeting up to a 1,000x increase in AI performance compared to the MI300X introduced in 2023. The MI500 series uses next-generation AMD CDNA 6 architecture, 2nm process technology, and HBM4E memory. CEO Lisa Su confirmed active customer engagement on MI500. The MI500 series targets 2027 production, positioning AMD to compete against NVIDIA's next-generation successor to Vera Rubin.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity Personal Computer is generally available on Mac since May 7 and coming to Windows in July 2026 with a hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator. Comet Enterprise is available with MDM deployment. CEO Aravind Srinivas has reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target. ARR: $500M (+335% YoY); 100M+ monthly active users; $20B valuation. CoreWeave's July 1 selloff does not affect Perplexity's dedicated GB200 NVL72 inference contract.

items.headline: Personal Computer GA on Mac; Windows + hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator coming July 2026
items.detail: Perplexity Personal Computer — an always-on AI agent running on a dedicated Mac mini that merges local files, apps, and sessions with Perplexity's cloud models — has been generally available on Mac for all Pro users since May 7. A Windows version with a hybrid local-cloud inference orchestrator is targeting July 2026: sensitive data stays on-device, compute-intensive tasks route to frontier cloud models. Intel Core Ultra 3 and NVIDIA RTX Spark are compatible. Comet browser is available for Enterprise with MDM deployment across macOS and Windows.

items.headline: 2028 IPO reaffirmed; $500M ARR; 100M MAU; $20B valuation; $750M Azure infrastructure deal
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas reaffirmed a 2028 IPO target, stating Perplexity is not accelerating its timeline despite Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. Apple-Perplexity acquisition discussions are confirmed dead. ARR reached $500 million (+335% YoY) with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20 billion valuation. A $750M Azure deal provides cloud infrastructure. Perplexity Computer runs as native side panels in Microsoft 365 apps. CoreWeave's multi-year inference partnership uses dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 clusters with Rubin systems rolling in H2 2026.

items.headline: CoreWeave GB200 NVL72 inference contract unaffected by July 1 CRWV selloff
items.detail: CoreWeave's 14% stock drop on July 1 on Meta cloud competition fears does not affect Perplexity's multi-year strategic inference partnership providing dedicated NVIDIA GB200 NVL72-powered clusters (announced March 4, 2026). NVIDIA Rubin-based systems are scheduled to roll into the partnership in H2 2026. The contract includes Weights & Biases Weave integration for model experimentation workflows. Perplexity's dedicated cluster is insulated from spot-market pricing volatility.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CRWV fell 14% to $85.68 on July 1 — its worst single day since IPO — after Bloomberg reported Meta is building a competing cloud business. Evercore ISI maintained an Outperform/$150 target noting the $21B Meta take-or-pay contract through 2032 insulates near-term revenue. CRWV is recovering on July 2. Revenue backlog: $99.4B (+50% QoQ). FY2026 revenue guidance: $12–13B. Fable 5 restoration means full Anthropic Claude traffic is resuming on CoreWeave infrastructure.

items.headline: CRWV fell 14% to $85.68 on July 1 — recovering July 2; Evercore ISI maintains Outperform/$150
items.detail: CoreWeave shares fell 14% to $85.68 on July 1 — the stock's worst single day since its March 2025 IPO — following Bloomberg's report of Meta's competing cloud plans. Evercore ISI reiterated an Outperform/$150 price target, arguing the selloff overreacts: the $21B Meta contract has take-or-pay characteristics, meaning Meta cannot redirect spend without penalty. The 37-analyst average Buy rating with a $143.41 price target remains intact. CRWV is recovering on July 2 from oversold conditions.

items.headline: ARIA enters public preview — AI Research and Iteration Agent for Weights & Biases
items.detail: CoreWeave launched ARIA (AI Research & Iteration Agent) into public preview on June 30, built into Weights & Biases using W&B Weave. ARIA works through thousands of experiment runs and tens of thousands of metrics in minutes, surfacing insights and recommending model improvements. W&B Weave's agent development capabilities also entered general availability. Fable 5's restoration on July 1 means full Anthropic Claude inference traffic can resume on CoreWeave's Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters after 19 days of suspension.

items.headline: Revenue backlog $99.4B (+50% QoQ); FY2026 guidance $12–13B; $18–19B exit run rate
items.detail: CoreWeave reaffirmed FY2026 revenue guidance of $12–13 billion with an $18–19 billion exit run rate. Revenue backlog surged 50% sequentially to $99.4 billion, anchored by the $21B Meta agreement (December 2032) and major contracts with Anthropic, Perplexity, and nine of the top ten AI model providers. 2026 capex floor: $31 billion. Jane Street committed approximately $6 billion to CoreWeave's AI cloud platform. NVIDIA holds approximately an 11% equity stake.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs) missed its end-of-June target and has not officially opened as of July 2. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10MW inference facility targets Q3 2026. Equity funding discussions are ongoing at approximately €20B ($23.2B) valuation targeting a €3B raise. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri integration.

items.headline: Bruyères-le-Châtel Paris data center still pending as of July 2 — targeting early July opening
items.detail: Mistral AI's primary data center at Bruyères-le-Châtel near Paris — 44MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems providing approximately 276 exaflops of FP4 compute, operated by Eclairion — has not officially opened as of July 2 after missing its end-of-June target. A secondary Les Ulis (Essonne) 10MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. Mistral Compute, a sovereign AI GPU cloud built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems, launched at VivaTech 2026 on June 20. Combined with a $1.4B Sweden investment, Mistral targets 200MW across Europe by end of 2027.

items.headline: €3B equity raise at ~€20B ($23.2B) valuation in progress; €1B 2026 revenue target
items.detail: Mistral AI is in discussions to raise approximately €3 billion ($3.5 billion) at a potential valuation near €20 billion ($23.2 billion), per Bloomberg. CEO Arthur Mensch targets €1 billion in 2026 revenue, up from €200 million in 2025. Mistral Medium 3.5, Mistral OCR 4, Mistral Small 4, and Voxtral TTS are among recent model releases. Industrial launch customers for the Vibe agent platform include Airbus, BMW Group, and ASML. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition.

items.headline: Apple evaluating Mistral as additional Siri model option — potential major distribution catalyst
items.detail: Reports indicate Apple is evaluating Mistral AI as an additional model option alongside Gemini for Siri and iOS integration, reflecting Apple's ongoing multi-provider hedging strategy. A Mistral-Apple distribution arrangement would be a significant enterprise channel for Mistral given Apple's installed base of more than 1.5 billion active devices. No official announcement has been made. The evaluation comes as Gemini 3.5 Pro delivery delays continue to pressure Apple's September 2026 Siri AI launch timeline.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Tesla's Q2 2026 delivery report was due today (July 2) against a Wall Street consensus of 406,024 vehicles. TSLA stock has been trading in the $400–$420 range. Optimus Gen 3 production is targeting late July/August with a goal of 50,000 units by year-end. The Cybercab active fleet spans approximately 60–100 vehicles across four cities with a sub-$30,000 customer price targeted within six months. Grok 4.5 is in private beta inside Tesla.

items.headline: Tesla Q2 2026 delivery report released today — consensus 406,024 vehicles
items.detail: Tesla released its Q2 2026 delivery report today (July 2) against a Wall Street consensus of 406,024 vehicles. Goldman Sachs raised its forecast ahead of the release, suggesting a possible beat. TSLA stock has been trading in the $400–$420 range. Tesla delivered 495,570 vehicles in Q1 2026. The Q2 figure will be closely watched as an indicator of Cybercab production ramp and traditional Model Y/3 demand alongside the expanding robotaxi fleet.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 production begins late July/August — 50,000 unit goal; external B2B late 2026
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot production is targeting late July/August 2026 with a full-year production goal of 50,000 units. Several thousand Optimus units are already operating in Tesla's own factories. First external commercial B2B customers are targeted for late 2026. Tesla's Fremont facility is converting Model S/X lines to robot assembly while Giga Texas North Campus targets 10 million robots per year long-term. Tesla's Terafab initiative targets in-house semiconductor fabrication for the AI5 chip.

items.headline: Cybercab fleet at ~60–100 units across 4 cities; under $30K price target in 6 months; Grok 4.5 in beta
items.detail: The Cybercab active fleet spans approximately 60–100+ units across Austin, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. A Tesla executive confirmed the Cybercab will be sold to customers for under $30,000 within six months, targeting volume deliveries before year-end 2026. EPA filing (June 19) confirmed production Cybercab specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range. FSD v14.3 is deployed with 20% reduced inference latency. Grok 4.5 (1.5T parameter V9 model) is running in private beta inside Tesla.

ipoTracker.status: October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; Series H $65B at $965B post-money; ARR $47B; Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls lifted July 1 — key pre-IPO operational risk removed
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, targeting an October 2026 Nasdaq IPO led by Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley with an expected raise of $60B+. The $65B Series H closed at a $965B post-money valuation, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, with $15B from hyperscalers including $5B from Amazon, plus Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. ARR: $47B as of May 2026. Fable 5 and Mythos 5 export controls lifted July 1 remove the largest pre-IPO operational risk that emerged in June. Claude Sonnet 5 launched July 1 as new Free/Pro default. Claude Science grants open through July 15.
ipoTracker.note: July 2 is the first full day without export control overhangs. Claude Science and Sonnet 5 launches strengthen the pre-IPO product narrative. October 2026 Nasdaq target remains intact.

latestArticle.title: Redeploying Claude Fable 5

ipoTracker.status: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna restricted to ~20 orgs; broader access expected July 10–17; IPO leaning 2027; 42-state AG probe ongoing; ChatGPT market share 46.4% — first below 50%
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 with the SEC in May/June 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading. The company is leaning toward a 2027 listing — CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility as reasons not to rush. GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna remain restricted to approximately 20 government-vetted organizations as of July 2; broader access expected July 10–17. A coalition of 42 state AGs opened a formal probe June 12. ChatGPT's global AI assistant market share fell to 46.4% (Gemini at 27.7%, Claude at 10.3%). Revenue: approximately $2B per month; projected $14B operating loss 2026.
ipoTracker.note: July 10–17 window for GPT-5.6 broader access and Cerebras Sol deployment are the next positive catalysts. 2027 IPO posture remains the working assumption.

latestArticle.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model

ipoTracker.status: SPCX $170.86 on July 1 (+4.06%); Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 payments started July 1; Grok 4.5 private beta at Tesla/SpaceX; $60B Cursor acquisition Q3 close on track; CSAM regulatory risk unresolved

Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16 following the Cursor announcement and pulled back to $170.86 by July 1 (+4.06% that day). Reflection AI's $150M/month Colossus 2 deal started July 1, marking both Colossus facilities now fully committed to third parties. Grok 4.5 (1.5 trillion parameters, V9 model) in private beta at Tesla and SpaceX. A 2T model is in final training expected to complete late July. The $60B Cursor acquisition is on track for Q3 2026 close.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: SPCX at $170.86 is recovering from post-ATH pullback. Both Colossus facilities now commercialized. CSAM disclosure and Cursor Q3 close are the key pending items.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI considering legal action against Apple over Siri integration

partnershipTracker.type: AI model integration — Gemini powering Siri AI
partnershipTracker.status: Phase 1 active; full deployment September 2026; Gemini 3.5 Pro still delayed as of July 2 — ongoing delivery risk to September launch window
partnershipTracker.terms: Apple struck a reported $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI, announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8). Phase 1 is in iOS 26.5; full Gemini Siri launches with iOS 27 in September 2026. GA September 2026; EU launch delayed pending DMA regulatory clarity. Gemini 3.5 Pro remains unreleased as of July 2 with no confirmed date — the highest-priority delivery risk for the Apple-Google relationship heading into Q3.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Reported by credible outlets; not officially confirmed by Apple or Google
partnershipTracker.note: Gemini 3.5 Pro delay is the highest-priority watch item for this relationship. No confirmed July date as of today.

latestArticle.title: Google delays Gemini 3.5 Pro to July as talent exodus deepens

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute and cloud GPU partnership
partnershipTracker.status: Active; MI300X on Azure; Copilot Cowork billing activated July 1 adds Azure AI compute demand; AMD Advancing AI July 22–23
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD MI300X GPU instances are deployed on Azure for AI inference workloads. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ + Web IQ) is GA across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. Copilot Cowork billing activated July 1, driving additional Azure AI workload demand. M365 commercial pricing increases took effect July 1. AMD Advancing AI 2026 (July 22–23) may include additional Azure-related deployment disclosures.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Ongoing relationship; no new announced terms
partnershipTracker.note: Copilot Cowork billing active adds Azure AI compute demand. AMD Advancing AI July 22–23 is the next potential milestone.

latestArticle.title: AMD at Microsoft Build 2026

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed $60B, ~6GW multi-year; MI450 samples shipping to Meta; Meta Compute cloud announcement may accelerate AMD GPU deployments for external workloads; AMD Advancing AI July 22–23
partnershipTracker.terms: AMD and Meta's 6-gigawatt, roughly $60 billion strategic partnership covers multiple generations of AMD Instinct GPUs co-developed via the Helios architecture through the Open Compute Project. AMD CEO confirmed MI450 samples are shipping to Meta. The agreement includes a 160-million-share AMD warrant giving Meta a potential 10% stake. Meta's Meta Compute cloud business announcement may accelerate AMD-Meta GPU deployments as Meta scales external cloud offerings.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies; samples shipping per AMD CEO
partnershipTracker.note: Meta Compute announcement may accelerate AMD-Meta GPU deployments at scale. AMD Advancing AI July 22–23 may include Meta deployment specifics.

latestArticle.title: AMD and Meta Announce Expanded Strategic Partnership to Deploy 6 Gigawatts of AMD GPUs

partnershipTracker.type: Infrastructure equity investment and multi-generational GPU supply
partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed deep strategic partnership; CRWV fell 14% on July 1 on Meta cloud news; recovering July 2; Fable 5 restored — full Anthropic Claude traffic to CoreWeave resumes
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA invested roughly $2 billion in CoreWeave Class A common stock (~11% stake) and committed to accelerating more than 5GW of AI factories by 2030. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up on June 1. Backlog $99.4 billion (+50% QoQ). CRWV fell 14% to $85.68 on July 1 on Meta cloud news — Evercore ISI maintains Outperform/$150. Fable 5 restoration means full Anthropic Claude traffic resumes on CoreWeave Vera Rubin NVL72 clusters.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies
partnershipTracker.note: Meta cloud announcement is the key negative development for CRWV stock but does not change the GPU supply relationship with NVIDIA. Fable 5 restoration is a utilization positive. CRWV recovering July 2.

latestArticle.title: CoreWeave Stock Drops Sharply on Report of Meta AI Cloud Business

partnershipTracker.status: Confirmed; Mistral Compute sovereign GPU cloud on 18K Grace Blackwell systems live; Paris data center still pending; Apple evaluating Mistral
partnershipTracker.terms: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20). Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. Mistral's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris has not officially opened as of July 2 after missing its end-of-June target. Apple is reportedly evaluating Mistral for Siri/iOS integration.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by both companies at VivaTech
partnershipTracker.note: Paris data center opening expected early July. Apple's Mistral evaluation is a potential major new distribution relationship.

latestArticle.title: France Advances Europe's AI Future With NVIDIA Technologies

partnershipTracker.type: AI compute contract — Colossus 2 data center access
partnershipTracker.status: Active as of July 1 — $150M/month payments began; Colossus 2 dedicated to Reflection AI through 2029
partnershipTracker.terms: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI (valued at $25 billion, working with DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI) is now active as of July 1 at $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access via Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Both Colossus 1 (Anthropic) and Colossus 2 (Reflection AI) are now fully committed to third parties.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by CNBC reporting; payments began July 1
partnershipTracker.note: July 1 payments began. With both Colossus facilities committed to third parties, xAI's own future dedicated compute access for Grok model training remains an open question.

latestArticle.title: SpaceX AI Colossus data center signs deal with Reflection AI

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute; AVGO down ~16.5% past month; six custom silicon customers confirmed
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo and Blackstone lead a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. The platform is operational since June 9. Jalapeño (the OpenAI-Broadcom custom chip unveiled June 24) confirms OpenAI as Broadcom's sixth custom-silicon customer. AVGO is down approximately 16.5% over the past month — does not change the platform terms.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: AVGO stock decline is market-driven. Six custom-silicon customers confirmed. FY2027 $100B+ AI revenue target is the long-term anchor.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI and Broadcom reveal Jalapeño, first AI chip in partnership
